stories.headline: Rubio Meets Pope Leo Amid Growing Tensions Between Trump and Vatican
stories.summary: The meeting at the Vatican followed President Trump’s condemnation of Pope Leo XIV for opposing the war in Iran.

stories.headline: Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
stories.summary: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting because of the impasse in talks with Hamas.

stories.headline: US underlines 'strong relationship' with Vatican as Rubio meets pope after Trump criticism
stories.summary: Rubio's visit comes after US President Trump accused the pope of being "weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy" after he made critical comments about the Iran war.

stories.headline: Iran considering US proposal as Trump says war will be 'over quickly'
stories.summary: Mediator Pakistan says it is "endeavouring to convert this ceasefire into a permanent end to this war".

stories.headline: Vatican stresses need for peace as Rubio meets pope amid strained relations

stories.headline: Shell latest oil giant to see profits surge due to Iran war impact
stories.summary: The energy giant's profits jump by nearly a quarter as it benefits from the recent volatility in the oil price

stories.headline: Not Everything Should Be Packaged As an ETF: Legum
stories.summary: Maital Legum, head of ETF solutions at Teucrium, joins Scarlet Fu and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss the development of exchange traded products as US stocks hit record highs. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US Awaits Iranian Response on Deal as Israel Strikes Lebanon
stories.summary: The US is waiting on Iran to respond to its proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end a war that’s killed thousands of people, with tensions still high in both the Persian Gulf and in Lebanon.

stories.headline: Tennessee Approves New Map Aimed at Flipping the Last Democratic Seat
stories.summary: After a Supreme Court ruling that weakened the Voting Rights Act of 1965, Republicans carved up a majority-Black Memphis seat as the national redistricting wars continue.

stories.headline: Rubio visits Rome aiming to repair White House ties with Pope Leo
stories.summary: Relations between the United States and the Vatican are at a low point over President Donald Trump’s attacks on Leo, who is a leading critic of the war in Iran.

stories.headline: Can China help end the Iran war?
stories.summary: Chinese and Iranian foreign ministers meet while a tense ceasefire holds.

stories.headline: Brazil’s Lula meets Trump amid efforts to avert new US trade tariffs

stories.headline: Lebanon Latest Israeli strikes Beirut despite ‘ceasefire’
stories.summary: Israel has carried out its first airstrike on Beirut’s southern suburbs since the April 16 US-brokered ceasefire.

stories.headline: Europe needs to be more radical on the energy crisis. But how? EU Climate Commissioner explains
stories.summary: The only way out of energy crises fueled by the wars in Iran and Ukraine is homegrown energy, EU Climate Commissioner Wopke Hoekstra told Euronews. "We owe it to our citizens and our companies."

stories.headline: Middle East war will slow down economic growth in Africa as cost of living crisis soars, IMF warns
stories.summary: According to the IMF's report, regional growth is expected to decline by 0.3 percentage points, and some 20 million people in Africa now face the risk of food insecurity as the Iran war raises energy and food prices.

stories.headline: 'Companies should not be regulated twice': EU reaches tentative deal to simplify AI rules
stories.summary: EU states and the Parliament reached an agreement to simplify the AI laws for businesses. Here’s a look at what has changed.

stories.headline: Rosenberg: Russia's Victory Day parade with no tanks a sign Ukraine war not going to plan
stories.summary: For the first time in nearly two decades, there will be no military hardware, just soldiers during Saturday's parade in Moscow.

stories.headline: Ukraine war 'led me to surrogacy' to earn money but a new law could end those plans

stories.headline: Charlotte, N.C. Mayor Vi Lyles Says She Will Resign in May
stories.summary: Mayor Vi Lyles did not fully explain why she would leave office early, saying only that she wanted to spend more time with family.

stories.headline: Susan Collins Says She Has Long Had a Benign Tremor

stories.summary: The FBI’s seizure of Fulton County’s voting materials has stoked alarm among election officials and democracy advocates.

stories.headline: Economic growth in the Pacific could slow as energy disruptions spread, ADB warns
stories.summary: Rising fuel and fertiliser import costs are placing growing pressure on economies across Asia and the Pacific, especially small island states that rely heavily on imports. The Asian Development Bank (ADB) says governments are accelerating energy transition and resilience measures in response.

stories.headline: Rwandan singer dies as he was being released from prison
stories.summary: The former university lecturer was a vocal critic of the government and ruling Rwandan Patriotic Front party.

stories.headline: China announces suspended death sentences for former defence ministers

stories.headline: Disturbances and 127 arrests mar Paris party after PSG Champions League victory

stories.headline: DR Congo president hints at extending his term and delaying polls

stories.headline: Top BJP leader's aide shot dead in violence after Indian state election

stories.headline: Colombia Buys Dollars in Spot Market as Swap Payment Looms
stories.summary: Colombia’s finance ministry is once again buying dollars in the spot market, traders say, as authorities gear up to pay off a controversial Swiss franc swap before the presidential election later this month.

stories.headline: Fidelity, Schwab to Roll Out Fees For Certain ETFs

They discuss recent reports that Fidelity Investments and Charles Schwab will start charging platform fees for the trading of certain ETFs.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Bolivia Is Selling $1 Billion in Bonds After Four-Year Break
stories.summary: Bolivia is returning to international capital markets for the first time in four years, as its new market-friendly government seizes on a tightening sovereign spread to raise fresh financing.

stories.headline: Genco Shipping CEO: Dry Bulk Sees Best Quarter Since 2022
stories.summary: Dry bulk shipping is booming again. Genco CEO John Wobensmith joins Bloomberg Open Interest to break down why this is the strongest quarter since 2022, how slower vessel speeds are quietly tightening supply, and why rising energy demand, fueled by AI and global power needs is unexpectedly driving the coal trade higher. (Corrects name of guest in description.) (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Ecuador Regulators Probe Major Bank in Years-Old Laundering Case

stories.headline: Fed’s Collins Agreed With FOMC Dissenters Over Statement

stories.summary: The immigrant detention center in the Everglades, which Gov. Ron DeSantis, a Republican, opened last July, may be too expensive to keep operating.

stories.headline: Singapore introduces caning for boys who bully others at school

stories.summary: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.

stories.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.
